flashcards.back: Knuth-Morris-Pratt algorithm, named after Donald Knuth, James Morris, and Vaughan Pratt who independently discovered it in 1977.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of KMP algorithm?
flashcards.back: O(n + m) where n is the length of text and m is the length of pattern.

flashcards.front: What is the space complexity of KMP algorithm?
flashcards.back: O(m) for storing the LPS (Longest Proper Prefix which is also Suffix) array.

flashcards.front: What is the LPS array in KMP?
flashcards.back: LPS (Longest Proper Prefix which is also Suffix) array stores the length of the longest proper prefix that is also a suffix for each position in the pattern.

flashcards.front: What are the two phases of KMP algorithm?
flashcards.back: 1. Preprocessing phase: Compute LPS array from the pattern in O(m) time. 2. Searching phase: Scan the text using LPS to find matches in O(n) time.

flashcards.front: Why is KMP better than naive string matching?
flashcards.back: KMP never re-examines characters in the text that have already been matched, while naive approach may backtrack and re-examine characters, giving O(nm) worst case.

flashcards.front: What is a proper prefix in KMP context?
flashcards.back: A proper prefix is a prefix that is not equal to the entire string itself. For example, for 'ABAB', proper prefixes are 'A', 'AB', 'ABA'.

flashcards.front: How does KMP handle overlapping matches?
flashcards.back: KMP efficiently handles overlapping matches by using the LPS array to determine the next position to compare without backtracking in the text.

flashcards.front: What is the worst-case scenario for KMP?
flashcards.back: The worst case occurs when the pattern consists of repeated characters (e.g., 'AAAAAAA') and the text also has many matches, still O(n + m) time complexity.

flashcards.front: What is the key insight behind KMP algorithm?
flashcards.back: When a mismatch occurs, we use the LPS array to skip characters in the pattern that have already been matched, avoiding redundant comparisons.
LPS is computed using dynamic programming-like approach: for each position, if characters match, extend the previous LPS value; otherwise, fall back to the previous LPS value.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between prefix and suffix?
flashcards.back: Prefix starts from the beginning of the string, suffix ends at the end of the string. A string can have both prefix and suffix properties simultaneously.

flashcards.front: Does KMP require backtracking in the text?
flashcards.back: No, KMP never backtracks in the text. It only moves forward, making exactly n comparisons during the searching phase.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of preprocessing in KMP?
flashcards.back: Preprocessing builds the LPS array which stores information about the pattern, enabling O(1) decision making during the search phase to skip comparisons.

flashcards.front: What is a real-world application of KMP?
flashcards.back: KMP is used in text editors for find-and-replace, DNA sequence matching in bioinformatics, network intrusion detection systems, and plagiarism detection.
